ansatz: use a smooth radial bump and dyadic rescalings to cover Fourier annuli
classification: standard partition algebra without independent cutoff-source closure
note: The usual radial bump package supplies the formal partition checks, but its normalization is useful here only after the desired cutoff-source role is assumed.

ansatz: choose compactly supported annular cutoffs with finite overlap
classification: annular cover with support imposed as source data
note: Compact annuli give a finite-overlap cover, but the exact identity and source-bearing support are not derived without imposing the cutoff support.

ansatz: use heat-kernel differences to define scale-local smooth cutoffs
classification: scale smoothing without compact annular bump partition
note: Heat-kernel differences provide smooth scale localization, but they are a regularization route rather than a radial bump partition with a closed identity.

ansatz: mollify sharp annular indicators to build a smooth partition
classification: mollified annuli with endpoint identity and overlap leakage
note: Mollification removes sharp edges, but the exact partition identity and uniform overlap bound are not obtained without tuning to the target shell collapse.

ansatz: declare the cutoff supports and partition identity directly
note: The candidate passes formal partition checks only by placing the support and identity in the hypotheses, so it is circular.
